cross_monitor_escalation_flags.summary: GPT-5.5 agentic tool suite (computer use, hosted shell, web search) expands operational surface for FIMI; universal jailbreak collapse per Stanford Index
US-China frontier gap narrows to 2.7%; GPT-5.5 agentic capabilities in widely deployed API raise dual-use risk
EU AI transparency obligations potentially delayed to 2027; jailbreak safety collapse across models
US AI researcher inflow drops 80% in one year; EU DMA review defers AI scope expansion preserving US hyperscaler dominance

analyst_briefing: No capability tier reviews possible absent capability_registry. OpenAI demonstrates increased capability tempo with GPT-5.5 API release (Confirmed, two Tier 1 sources), third GPT-5 model in eight weeks featuring 1M context and agentic tools, compressing safety evaluation windows. EU AI Act faces slippage via Digital Omnibus proposal delaying high-risk obligations to 2027–2028 (Confirmed, Tier 1 EC sources), matching Standards Vacuum and Governance Fragmentation vectors; AI-generated content Code remains on_track for May–June finalisation. Stanford HAI 2026 Index (Confirmed) evidences 55% AI incident rise, universal jailbreak collapse, US-China model gap at 2.7%, and 80% US researcher inflow drop—key geopolitical signals. Risk matches on fragmentation, disinfo, platform power; no cyber escalations. Escalations: fcw/scem/wdm (MEDIUM) on agentic/jailbreak risks; esa (HIGH) on talent/dependency. No contested findings.
